article_meta.title: | Updated November 25, 2025 9:00am EST Nearly 1 in 4 American households

reasoning: The summary remains neutral, attributes data to the Bank of America Institute, avoids loaded language, and does not adopt any sensational framing. Minor un-attributed phrasing about “lifestyle creep” is present but does not materially shift tone.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and descriptors throughout; no charged phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Most claims are clearly tied to the BofA Institute; a causal note on lifestyle creep is not explicitly attributed in one sentence.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Slightly presents lifestyle creep as general fact rather than attribution, but otherwise avoids subjective judgments.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Single-source data is presented proportionally; no undue emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: All key perspectives present (BofA Institute and its economist) are included.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are descriptive and factual, not value-laden.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects the article’s balance without amplifying negatives.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege or dismiss any group beyond citing the report.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than any potential framing; sticks to data and definitions.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures the report, the economist’s commentary, and the outlet’s reporting as sources.
